compose_config.stdout:
name: codex
services:
  app:
    command:
      - sh
      - -c
      - pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt && uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
    container_name: target-app
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
        required: true
    environment:
      DB_HOST: db
      DB_NAME: appdb
      DB_PASSWORD: apppassword
      DB_PORT: "3306"
      DB_USER: appuser
    image: python:3.12-slim
    networks:
      default: null
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - type: bind
        source: /Users/ryoike/Documents/codex/app
        target: /app
        bind: {}
    working_dir: /app
  db:
    container_name: target-db
    environment:
      MYSQL_DATABASE: appdb
      MYSQL_PASSWORD: apppassword
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: rootpassword
      MYSQL_USER: appuser
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD-SHELL
        - mysqladmin ping -h 127.0.0.1 -uroot -p$$MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD
      timeout: 3s
      interval: 5s
      retries: 20
      start_period: 20s
    image: mysql:8.4
    networks:
      default: null
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - type: volume
        source: mysql_data
        target: /var/lib/mysql
        volume: {}
      - type: bind
        source: /Users/ryoike/Documents/codex/db/init.sql
        target: /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql
        read_only: true
        bind: {}
  nginx:
    container_name: target-nginx
    depends_on:
      app:
        condition: service_started
        required: true
    image: nginx:1.27-alpine
    networks:
      default: null
    ports:
      - mode: ingress
        target: 80
        published: "8080"
        protocol: tcp
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - type: bind
        source: /Users/ryoike/Documents/codex/nginx/nginx.conf
        target: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
        read_only: true
        bind: {}
networks:
  default:
    name: codex_default
volumes:
  mysql_data:
    name: codex_mysql_data

planner_summary: The Nginx configuration for the 'backend' upstream is pointing to port 8001, but the 'app' service is listening on port 8000. This mismatch causes Nginx to fail connecting to the upstream, resulting in 502 errors. The fix is to update the Nginx configuration to use the correct port for the 'app' service and then restart Nginx.
